name: Calculator: Basic addition
description: Basic calculator tool usage - add two numbers.
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

result = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'add', {'a': 10, 'b': 20})
print(f'Result: {result}')
prompt: Add 10 and 20 using the calculator tool. Call calculator.add with a=10 and b=20, then print 'Result: {result}'.

name: Calculator: Complex expression
description: Evaluate a mathematical expression using calculator.
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

result = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'calculate', {'expression': '(100 + 200) * 3 - 50'})
print(f'Result: {result}')
prompt: Calculate (100 + 200) * 3 - 50 using the calculator tool. Call calculator.calculate with the expression, then print 'Result: {result}'.

name: Calculator: Sum of list
description: Sum a list of numbers using calculator.
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

numbers = [10, 20, 30, 40, 50]
total = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'sum_list', {'numbers': numbers})
print(f'Sum: {total}')
prompt: Sum the list [10, 20, 30, 40, 50] using calculator.sum_list. Print 'Sum: {total}'.

name: Calculator: Average calculation
description: Calculate average of a number list.
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

numbers = [10, 20, 30, 40, 50]
avg = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'avg_list', {'numbers': numbers})
print(f'Average: {avg:.2f}')
prompt: Calculate the average of [10, 20, 30, 40, 50] using calculator.avg_list. Print 'Average: {avg:.2f}'.

name: Calculator: Power and sqrt
description: Use power and square root operations.
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

power = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'power', {'base': 2, 'exponent': 10})
sqrt = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'sqrt', {'n': 144})
print(f'2^10={power}, sqrt(144)={sqrt:.0f}')
prompt: Calculate 2^10 using calculator.power and sqrt(144) using calculator.sqrt. Print '2^10={power}, sqrt(144)={sqrt:.0f}'.

name: Weather: Get temperature
description: Get current weather for a location.
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

weather = call_mcp_tool('weather', 'get_weather', {'location': 'Berlin', 'units': 'celsius'})
print(f"Temperature: {weather['temperature']}°C")
prompt: Get weather for Berlin in celsius using weather.get_weather. Print 'Temperature: {temp}°C'.

name: Weather: Get forecast

print(f"Forecast days: {len(forecast['forecast'])}")
prompt: Get a 3-day weather forecast for Paris using weather.get_forecast. Print 'Forecast days: {count}'.

name: Text processing: Search and replace
description: Search for text and replace it.
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

text = 'Hello World, welcome to the World of PTC'
found = call_mcp_tool('text', 'search', {'text': text, 'pattern': 'World'})
result = call_mcp_tool('text', 'replace', {'text': text, 'old': 'World', 'new': 'Universe'})
print(f'Found: {found}, Result: {result}')
expected_output: Found: True, Result: Hello Universe, welcome to the Universe of PTC
prompt: Search for 'World' in the text, replace with 'Universe' using text.search and text.replace. Print 'Found: {found}, Result: {result}'.

name: Text processing: Regex matching

print(f'Emails found: {len(emails)}')
prompt: Find all email addresses in the text using text.regex_findall with pattern r'[a-z]+@[a-z]+\.[a-z]+'. Print 'Emails found: {count}'.

name: Math: Prime check
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

numbers = [7, 10, 13, 20, 23]
results = []
for n in numbers:
    is_p = call_mcp_tool('math', 'is_prime', {'n': n})
    results.append((n, is_p))
primes = [n for n, p in results if p]
print(f'Primes: {primes}')
prompt: Check which of [7, 10, 13, 20, 23] are prime using math.is_prime. Print 'Primes: {list}'.

name: Math: GCD and LCM
reference_code:

lcm = call_mcp_tool('math', 'lcm', {'a': 4, 'b': 6})
print(f'GCD(48,18)={gcd}, LCM(4,6)={lcm}')
prompt: Calculate GCD of 48 and 18, and LCM of 4 and 6. Print 'GCD(48,18)={gcd}, LCM(4,6)={lcm}'.

name: Transform: Sort data

prompt: Sort the data by 'score' descending using transform.sort_by. Print 'Top: {name} with {score}' for the first item.

name: Hard: Multi-city weather comparison
description: Compare weather across multiple cities with calculations.
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

cities = ['Berlin', 'Paris', 'London', 'Tokyo', 'New York']
temps = []
for city in cities:
    w = call_mcp_tool('weather', 'get_weather', {'location': city, 'units': 'celsius'})
    temps.append(w['temperature'])
avg = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'avg_list', {'numbers': temps})
max_temp = max(temps)
min_temp = min(temps)
print(f'Avg: {avg:.1f}°C, Range: {min_temp}-{max_temp}°C')
prompt: Get weather for 5 cities, calculate average, min, max. Print 'Avg: {avg:.1f}°C, Range: {min}-{max}°C'.

name: Hard: Complex file processing
description: Read file, process with multiple tools.
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

content = call_mcp_tool('filesystem', 'read_file', {'path': '/workspace/data/data.csv'})
lines = call_mcp_tool('text', 'split', {'text': content, 'delimiter': '\n'})
values = []
for line in lines[1:]:
    if line:
        parts = call_mcp_tool('text', 'split', {'text': line, 'delimiter': ','})
        if len(parts) > 1:
            values.append(int(parts[1]))
total = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'sum_list', {'numbers': values})
print(f'Total: {total}')
prompt: Read CSV, parse values from second column, sum them. Print 'Total: {sum}'.

name: Hard: Complex text transformation
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

text = 'The Quick Brown Fox Jumps Over The Lazy Dog'
lower = call_mcp_tool('text', 'to_lower', {'text': text})
words = call_mcp_tool('text', 'split', {'text': lower, 'delimiter': ' '})
reversed_words = [call_mcp_tool('text', 'join', {'items': list(w)[::-1], 'delimiter': ''}) for w in words[:3]]
result = call_mcp_tool('text', 'join', {'items': reversed_words, 'delimiter': '-'})
print(f'Result: {result}')
prompt: Lowercase text, split, reverse first 3 words, join with '-'. Print 'Result: {result}'.

name: Hard: Email workflow
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

emails = call_mcp_tool('email', 'fetch', {})
from_addresses = [e['from'] for e in emails]
unique_senders = call_mcp_tool('transform', 'unique_values', {'data': [{'from': f} for f in from_addresses], 'field': 'from'})
body = call_mcp_tool('text', 'join', {'items': unique_senders, 'delimiter': ', '})
sent = call_mcp_tool('email', 'send', {'to': 'summary@example.com', 'subject': 'Summary', 'body': body})
print(f'Sent summary to {len(unique_senders)} unique senders: {sent["sent"]}')
expected_output: Sent summary to 3 unique senders: True
prompt: Fetch emails, get unique senders, join them, send summary. Print 'Sent summary to {count} unique senders: {sent}'.

name: Hard: Calendar scheduling
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

events = [('Meeting A', '2024-03-01'), ('Meeting B', '2024-03-02'), ('Meeting C', '2024-03-03')]
created = 0
for title, date in events:
    result = call_mcp_tool('calendar', 'create_event', {'title': title, 'date': date})
    if result['created']:
        created += 1
print(f'Created {created} events')
prompt: Create 3 events with different dates. Print 'Created {count} events'.

name: Hard: Historical weather analysis
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

history = call_mcp_tool('weather', 'get_historical', {'location': 'London', 'days_back': 7, 'units': 'celsius'})
temps = [day['temperature'] for day in history['history']]
avg = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'avg_list', {'numbers': temps})
trend = 'rising' if temps[-1] > temps[0] else 'falling'
print(f'7-day avg: {avg:.1f}°C, Trend: {trend}')
prompt: Get 7-day historical weather for London, calculate average temp, determine trend. Print '7-day avg: {avg:.1f}°C, Trend: {trend}'.

name: Hard: Prime factorization
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

n = 60
factors = []
primes = call_mcp_tool('math', 'primes_up_to', {'n': n})
remaining = n
for p in primes:
    while remaining % p == 0:
        factors.append(p)
        remaining = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'divide', {'a': remaining, 'b': p})
factor_str = call_mcp_tool('text', 'join', {'items': factors, 'delimiter': ' x '})
print(f'60 = {factor_str}')
prompt: Factor 60 into primes. Print '60 = {factors}' joined with ' x '.

name: Hard: Complex database query
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

# Get max salary
max_sal = call_mcp_tool('database', 'aggregate', {'table': 'users', 'type': 'max', 'column': 'salary'})
# Get min salary
min_sal = call_mcp_tool('database', 'aggregate', {'table': 'users', 'type': 'min', 'column': 'salary'})
# Calculate range
sal_range = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'subtract', {'a': max_sal, 'b': min_sal})
print(f'Salary range: ${sal_range}')
prompt: Calculate salary range (max - min) from users table. Print 'Salary range: ${range}'.

name: Expert: Multi-file analysis
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

keys = call_mcp_tool('filesystem', 'read_lines', {'path': '/workspace/data/keys.txt'})
values = call_mcp_tool('filesystem', 'read_lines', {'path': '/workspace/data/values.txt'})
pairs = []
for k, v in zip(keys, values):
    k_clean = call_mcp_tool('text', 'strip', {'text': k})
    v_clean = call_mcp_tool('text', 'strip', {'text': v})
    pairs.append(f'{k_clean}={v_clean}')
result = call_mcp_tool('text', 'join', {'items': pairs, 'delimiter': ', '})
print(f'Pairs: {result}')
prompt: Read keys.txt and values.txt, pair them line by line, join as 'key=value'. Print 'Pairs: {result}'.

name: Expert: Weather prediction
description: Predict weather based on historical data.
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

current = call_mcp_tool('weather', 'get_weather', {'location': 'Berlin', 'units': 'celsius'})
forecast = call_mcp_tool('weather', 'get_forecast', {'location': 'Berlin', 'days': 3, 'units': 'celsius'})
hist = call_mcp_tool('weather', 'get_historical', {'location': 'Berlin', 'days_back': 3, 'units': 'celsius'})

current_temp = current['temperature']
forecast_temps = [d['temperature'] for d in forecast['forecast']]
hist_temps = [d['temperature'] for d in hist['history']]
all_temps = hist_temps + [current_temp] + forecast_temps
avg = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'avg_list', {'numbers': all_temps})
print(f'7-day average (hist+curr+forecast): {avg:.1f}°C')
prompt: Combine historical (3 days), current, and forecast (3 days) temps, calculate average. Print '7-day average (hist+curr+forecast): {avg:.1f}°C'.

name: Expert: Data correlation
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

# Get user cities
users = call_mcp_tool('database', 'query', {'table': 'users', 'columns': ['city']})
cities = call_mcp_tool('transform', 'unique_values', {'data': users, 'field': 'city'})

# Get temps for each city
temps = []
for city in cities:
    w = call_mcp_tool('weather', 'get_weather', {'location': city, 'units': 'celsius'})
    temps.append(w['temperature'])

avg_temp = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'avg_list', {'numbers': temps})
print(f'Average temp across {len(cities)} user cities: {avg_temp:.1f}°C')
prompt: Get unique user cities, fetch weather for each, calculate average temp. Print 'Average temp across {count} user cities: {avg:.1f}°C'.

from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

lower = call_mcp_tool('text', 'to_lower', {'text': text})
words = call_mcp_tool('text', 'split', {'text': lower, 'delimiter': ' '})

name: Expert: Math sequence
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

# Get Fibonacci numbers up to 100
fib = call_mcp_tool('math', 'fibonacci', {'n': 12})
fib_under_100 = [x for x in fib if x < 100]

# Check which are prime
primes_in_fib = []
for n in fib_under_100:
    if n > 1 and call_mcp_tool('math', 'is_prime', {'n': n}):
        primes_in_fib.append(n)

print(f'Primes in Fibonacci (<100): {primes_in_fib}')
prompt: Get Fibonacci up to 100, find primes among them. Print 'Primes in Fibonacci (<100): {list}'.

name: Expert: Transform pipeline
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

# Get users
users = call_mcp_tool('database', 'query', {'table': 'users', 'columns': ['name', 'city', 'salary']})

# Group by city
by_city = call_mcp_tool('transform', 'group_by', {'data': users, 'key': 'city'})

# Calculate average salary per city
avg_by_city = {}
for city, city_users in by_city.items():
    salaries = call_mcp_tool('transform', 'map_field', {'data': city_users, 'field': 'salary'})
    avg = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'avg_list', {'numbers': salaries})
    avg_by_city[city] = int(avg)

print(f'Average salaries by city: {avg_by_city}')
prompt: Group users by city, calculate average salary per city. Print 'Average salaries by city: {dict}'.

name: Expert: Advanced filtering
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

# Filter users by age > 30 and salary > 60000
users = call_mcp_tool('database', 'query', {'table': 'users', 'columns': ['name', 'age', 'salary']})
filtered = [u for u in users if u['age'] > 30 and u['salary'] > 60000]

# Sort by salary descending
sorted_users = call_mcp_tool('transform', 'sort_by', {'data': filtered, 'key': 'salary', 'reverse': True})

names = call_mcp_tool('transform', 'map_field', {'data': sorted_users, 'field': 'name'})
print(f'High earners over 30: {names}')
prompt: Filter users where age>30 AND salary>60000, sort by salary desc, extract names. Print 'High earners over 30: {names}'.

name: Expert: Cross-tool analysis
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

# Get user count per city
users = call_mcp_tool('database', 'query', {'table': 'users', 'columns': ['city']})
user_counts = call_mcp_tool('transform', 'count_by', {'data': users, 'field': 'city'})

# Get temp per city and calculate users per degree
ratios = {}
for city, count in user_counts.items():
    w = call_mcp_tool('weather', 'get_weather', {'location': city, 'units': 'celsius'})
    temp = w['temperature']
    ratio = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'divide', {'a': count, 'b': temp})
    ratios[city] = round(ratio, 2)

print(f'Users per degree by city: {ratios}')
prompt: Count users per city, get weather, calculate users/temp ratio per city. Print 'Users per degree by city: {ratios}'.

name: Expert: Complete workflow
reference_code:
from mock_mcp_client import call_mcp_tool

# Read and parse
content = call_mcp_tool('filesystem', 'read_file', {'path': '/workspace/data/report.txt'})
lines = call_mcp_tool('text', 'split', {'text': content, 'delimiter': '\n'})

# Extract values
values = []
for line in lines:
    if ':' in line:
        parts = call_mcp_tool('text', 'split', {'text': line, 'delimiter': ': '})
        if len(parts) == 2:
            values.append(int(parts[1]))

# Calculate stats
total = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'sum_list', {'numbers': values})
avg = call_mcp_tool('calculator', 'avg_list', {'numbers': values})

# Send report
report = f'Total: {total}, Average: {avg:.1f}'
sent = call_mcp_tool('email', 'send', {'to': 'reports@example.com', 'subject': 'Quarterly Report', 'body': report})

print(f'Report sent: {sent["sent"]} with {len(values)} quarters')
prompt: Parse report.txt, extract Q values, calculate total and average, send email report. Print 'Report sent: {sent} with {count} quarters'.
